Track selection and reuse.question: How should the active track be selected and retained?
Track selection and reuse.answer: Persist an explicit current track, reuse it on the Drive screen, display its name clearly, and never silently switch to the newest track.
options_presented: Select a track before every session

Track selection and reuse.question: Where should users select the current track?
Track selection and reuse.answer: Provide a compact selector on Drive and a Set as current track action in the Review track detail screen.
options_presented: Drive selector plus Review detail action

Track selection and reuse.question: Should location-based nearby-track recommendation be included in Phase 5?
Track selection and reuse.answer: No. Phase 5 uses explicit manual selection and remembers the last selection.
options_presented: Recommend nearby locally saved tracks in Phase 5

Track selection and reuse.question: What happens when no timing-ready track is selected?
Track selection and reuse.answer: Block Timing and show a direct Select track action; do not fall back to the newest track.

Start/finish and sector editor.question: How are timing lines created and adjusted?
Start/finish and sector editor.answer: Start/finish uses the offline reference trace editor. Sector boundaries are generated by the system; users do not draw or reshape them.
options_presented: System-generated boundaries constrained to the reference line

Start/finish and sector editor.question: What does a sector represent?
Start/finish and sector editor.answer: A sector is a complete interval of the closed course, not a line. N sectors use N-1 intermediate boundaries plus the start/finish boundary, leaving no uncovered course segment.
options_presented: Independent timing lines without a final interval

Start/finish and sector editor.question: How many sectors can a track use?
Start/finish and sector editor.answer: Sector timing can be disabled or configured for 2 through 6 sectors; new configurations default to 3 sectors.

Start/finish and sector editor.question: How are sector boundaries positioned and edited?
Start/finish and sector editor.answer: Place N-1 boundaries at equal reference-line arc-length intervals. Generate each boundary as the normal to the local reference-line tangent. Users may drag only the position along the reference line; the system continuously recomputes the perpendicular line.

Track changes and historical data.question: Which edits update a track in place versus creating a revision?
Track changes and historical data.answer: Metadata-only edits such as the name update the profile directly. Changes to the reference line, start/finish, sector count, or sector positions create a new immutable revision under the same logical track profile.
options_presented: Every edit creates an unrelated track

Track changes and historical data.question: How are old revisions exposed?
Track changes and historical data.answer: Hide old revisions from the normal track selector, retain them for historical sessions, and expose them through version history in track details.
options_presented: Latest revision in normal selector with version history in details
Show every revision in the normal selector

Track changes and historical data.question: When can a ghost carry forward to a new revision?
Track changes and historical data.answer: Sector-only changes retain fastest-lap and ghost compatibility. Changes to the reference line, start/finish, or travel direction break compatibility and the new revision builds a new fastest lap.
options_presented: Never carry a ghost across revisions

Track changes and historical data.question: How do deletion and duplication affect history?
Track changes and historical data.answer: Delete means archive: remove the track from active selection while retaining sessions, revisions, and ghosts. Duplicate creates an independent track identity with separate future version history.

deferred_ideas: Recommend nearby tracks from the user's live latitude and longitude, with explicit confirmation before switching.
Future matching against tracks saved by other users remains outside Phase 5.
